name: skin_api_response
description: Optimize an API JSON response using AgentSkin SSS. Recursively prunes JSON to keep only signal-matched keys, applies semantic aliases, and flattens to markdown. Up to 88% token reduction on rich JSON APIs (GitHub, Stripe, npm, etc.).

data.description: JSON response data to optimize. Can be any JSON-serializable structure.

url.description: Original URL of the API. When provided, auto-classification matches against built-in API rule families (GitHub, npm, HackerNews, weather, etc.) to select the right signals/aliases.

signals.description: Field names to preserve. If empty and url is provided, signals are auto-selected from the matched rule. If empty and url is missing, uses DEFAULT_SIGNAL_KEYS.

autoClassify.description: If true and url is provided, auto-select signals/aliases from matched rule. If false, only use explicit signals/aliases.

stripAnsi.description: Strip ANSI escape codes from the JSON before parsing.

smallThreshold.description: Bypass optimization for payloads below this size (chars). Avoids overhead on already-compact responses.

compaction.description: CompactionMetadata with kinds array and authoritative flag.

rule.description: Matched URL rule, or null if no rule matched.

handler: import { skin } from '../../agentskin/backend/lib/skin-engine.js'; return await skin(args.data, { url: args.url, signals: args.signals, aliases: args.aliases, autoClassify: args.autoClassify, stripAnsi: args.stripAnsi, smallThreshold: args.smallThreshold });
